Entity_Description: Cedar is an unincorporated rural community in the Regional District of Nanaimo on the east coast of Vancouver Island, British Columbia, roughly 8 kilometres south of the city of Nanaimo. As of the 2021 Census it had a population of about 3,500. Set along the Strait of Georgia across from Gabriola Island, Cedar is characterized by farms, forested acreages and waterfront properties. The Nanaimo Airport lies just south at nearby Cassidy, and the area retains a distinctly agricultural, semi-rural character.

Long_Description:
Cedar unfolds south of Nanaimo as a patchwork of farmland, forest and shoreline that feels a world away from the city just up the highway. Head toward the water and the subdivisions give way to hobby farms, horse properties and the acreages of the Yellow Point area, where roadside stands and long driveways set the pace. Down at Cedar-by-the-Sea, older cottages and rebuilt view homes look across the Strait of Georgia toward Gabriola Island, while inland lots sit on well and septic beneath second-growth timber, a reminder of the region's forestry roots.

That mix shapes what {SERVICE} looks like here. A {BUSINESS_TYPE} working in Cedar has to plan for gravel lanes, spread-out rural addresses and the wet coastal climate that rolls in off the Pacific: months of steady rain that test roofs, drainage and any exterior work, followed by dry summers when the community swells with visitors heading to the water. {COMPANY_NAME} builds every {SERVICE} around those realities, so {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION} holds up to Cedar conditions rather than city assumptions.

The property mix is unusually broad for a community of roughly 3,500. Within a few kilometres you will find 1970s ranchers, working farms, waterfront homes and newer builds filling in near the Nanaimo Airport at Cassidy, plus the small commercial cluster that serves the surrounding countryside. Each calls for a different approach, and {COMPANY_NAME} tailors {SERVICE_LIST} to match, whether it is a heritage farmhouse near Yellow Point or a modern home overlooking the strait.

Cedar's position matters too. We are minutes from Nanaimo, close to Cassidy and Ladysmith, and a short hop from the Gabriola Island ferry, so our {BUSINESS_TYPE} crews cover the whole south-Nanaimo area without treating Cedar as an afterthought on the way somewhere else. That local focus means {RESPONSE_TIME} response, {ESTIMATE_TYPE}, and a team that already knows which neighbourhoods run on septic and how the seasons hit local {PROPERTY_TYPE}.

From the farms of Yellow Point to the shoreline of Cedar-by-the-Sea, {COMPANY_NAME} delivers {SERVICE} that respects how this community is built and how it lives. Call {PHONE} to get started.
Local_Facts:
- Population: approximately 3,500 (2021 Census)
- Regional District: Nanaimo; Cedar is an unincorporated community
- Location: about 8 km south of downtown Nanaimo on Vancouver Island's east coast
- Setting: fronts the Strait of Georgia, directly across from Gabriola Island
- The Nanaimo Airport is located just south of Cedar at nearby Cassidy
- Character: semi-rural mix of farms, forested acreages and waterfront homes, including the Yellow Point and Cedar-by-the-Sea areas
- Climate: mild, wet coastal climate with abundant winter rainfall and drier summers
